steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The blank follows the noun "list" and precedes "the Hawai'i Register of Historic Places." The proper name after the blank renames and specifies which list is meant, so the blank needs punctuation that introduces a renaming noun phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice in the sentence:
  • Choice A — 'list: the': a colon correctly introduces a noun phrase that specifies the preceding noun — "an important list: the Hawai'i Register of Historic Places" is a complete, valid sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'list. The': a period creates two sentences, but "The Hawai'i Register of Historic Places" alone is a fragment with no verb — violates the complete-sentence rule. ✗
  • Choice C — 'list; the': a semicolon must join two independent clauses, but "the Hawai'i Register of Historic Places" is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'list the': no punctuation leaves the two nouns strung together without showing their relationship, creating a run-on noun phrase. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
